ai_summary: Overall risk posture is elevated: two security groups (legacy-bastion and oops-all-open) expose administrative or all-port access directly to the internet, and prod-postgres-sg exposes a production database. Prioritize: 1) Close SSH/RDP on legacy-bastion and replace with SSM Session Manager. 2) Lock down oops-all-open immediately, it has zero port restriction. 3) Restrict prod-postgres-sg to the application subnet only. 4) Review old-unused-sg for deletion. 5) Migrate workloads off the default security group. A recurring pattern: groups without an Owner tag are disproportionately represented among the exposed findings, suggesting tagging enforcement would also improve accountability.

findings.title: SSH (port 22) open to the internet
findings.detail: Rule 'TCP/22' permits access from 0.0.0.0/0. Remote administrative shell access.
findings.remediation: Restrict source to specific IP ranges (e.g. office/VPN CIDR) or remove public access. Prefer a bastion host, AWS SSM Session Manager, or a VPN instead of exposing SSH directly.

findings.title: RDP (port 3389) open to the internet
findings.detail: Rule 'TCP/3389' permits access from 0.0.0.0/0. Windows remote desktop.
findings.remediation: Restrict source to specific IP ranges (e.g. office/VPN CIDR) or remove public access. Prefer a bastion host, AWS SSM Session Manager, or a VPN instead of exposing RDP directly.

findings.title: All ports open to the internet
findings.remediation: Restrict to only the specific ports and protocols required. Never allow all-ports access from 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0.

findings.title: PostgreSQL (port 5432) open to the internet
findings.detail: Rule 'TCP/5432' permits access from 0.0.0.0/0. Database engine port.
findings.remediation: Restrict source to specific IP ranges (e.g. office/VPN CIDR) or remove public access. Prefer a bastion host, AWS SSM Session Manager, or a VPN instead of exposing PostgreSQL directly.

findings.title: Default security group is actively in use
findings.detail: The default SG in VPC vpc-01234 is attached to 1 interface(s).
findings.remediation: Create purpose-specific security groups and migrate resources off the default SG.

findings.title: Security group appears unused
findings.detail: No network interfaces (ENIs) reference this security group.
findings.remediation: Verify, then remove if genuinely unused to reduce attack surface and configuration drift.

findings.title: Unrestricted outbound access (all ports/protocols to 0.0.0.0/0)
findings.detail: This is the AWS default egress rule. A compromised instance in this SG can freely exfiltrate data or reach any host.
findings.remediation: Consider scoping egress to required destinations/ports for sensitive workloads.

findings.title: No tags applied
findings.detail: This security group has no tags, making ownership, environment, and purpose unclear.
findings.remediation: Apply standard tags (Owner, Environment, Application) per your tagging policy.

findings.title: Unrestricted outbound access (all ports/protocols to 0.0.0.0/0)

findings.remediation: Apply standard tags (Owner, Environment, Application) per your tagging policy.

findings.title: Web port open to the internet (TCP/443)
findings.detail: Rule permits TCP/443 from 0.0.0.0/0. Common/expected for public web servers or load balancers — confirm this SG fronts a public resource.
findings.remediation: If attached to internal resources, restrict the source. If public-facing, ensure a WAF and rate limiting are in place.

findings.title: Web port open to the internet (TCP/80)
findings.detail: Rule permits TCP/80 from 0.0.0.0/0. Common/expected for public web servers or load balancers — confirm this SG fronts a public resource.
findings.remediation: If attached to internal resources, restrict the source. If public-facing, ensure a WAF and rate limiting are in place.
